title: Portuguese Pork Ribs
description: A classic Portuguese-inspired recipe featuring tender pork ribs, slow-cooked to perfection. These flavorful ribs are perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until caramelized, about 10 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork ribs and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the red wine, marmalade, and rosemary to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the ribs are tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Serve the ribs hot, garnished with additional rosemary if desired.
Strain the sauce from the pot and serve it on the side.
